question: How does multi-tenancy work end-to-end in this codebase? I specifically need to understand: (1) the Tenant model and how rows are scoped to a tenant (DB schema, middleware, repository layer), (2) how a request is associated with a tenant (subdomain? header? path? JWT claim?), (3) how tenant isolation is enforced in handlers and queries, (4) the white-label branding system — what is the Branding model, how is it loaded at runtime on the frontend (BrandingContext, BrandingThemeInjector), and how is it stored/served per-tenant on the backend. Cite concrete file paths.
answer:
The LastSaaS repository implements multi-tenancy end-to-end, from the database schema and backend request handling to the frontend's dynamic UI.

### 1. The Tenant Model and Row Scoping

*   **[`Tenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L9) Model:** The core of multi-tenancy is the [`Tenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L9) struct, defined in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/tenant.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go). This model includes fields such as [`ID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L38) (a [`primitive.ObjectID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L38)), [`Name`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L39), [`Slug`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L12), [`IsRoot`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L13) (to identify the system's root tenant), [`IsActive`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fuser.go#L30), and comprehensive billing details like [`PlanID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L166), [`StripeCustomerID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L19), [`BillingStatus`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Ftenant.go#L20), and credit allocations. The structure ensures tenant-specific configurations and financial information are maintained distinctly, as mentioned in [Multi-Tenancy and Membership Models](#core-data-models-and-validation-multi-tenancy-and-membership-models).

*   **Row Scoping (Database):** Data associated with a specific tenant generally includes a [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) field of type [`primitive.ObjectID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fplan.go#L38). For example, [`TenantMembership`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fmembership.go#L17) (defined in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/membership.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fmembership.go)) directly links a user ([`UserID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fauth%2Fjwt.go#L23)) to a [`TenantID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fstripe%2Fstripe.go#L172). Similarly, [`SystemLog`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fsystem_log.go#L30) entries can be scoped by [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46). When querying, this [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) is used in [`bson.M`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L786) filters to ensure isolation, as seen in various handlers like [`ListTransactions`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbilling.go#L375) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/billing.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbilling.go) or [`GetActivity`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Ftenant.go#L520) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/tenant.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Ftenant.go).

*   **Middleware:** The backend uses [`middleware.NewTenantMiddleware`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L255) to extract tenant information from incoming requests and make it available to subsequent handlers, ensuring that all operations are performed within the context of the correct tenant (see [Middleware Implementation for Cross-Cutting Concerns](#http-server-and-api-gateway-middleware-implementation-for-cross-cutting-concerns)).

*   **Repository Layer:** The [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/db`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fdb) package manages MongoDB connections and collections. Queries within handlers explicitly include [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) in their filters to scope results to the active tenant. For instance, in `handlers/tenant.go`, `h.db.TenantMemberships().Find(r.Context(), bson.M{"tenantId": tenant.ID})` is used to list members for the current tenant.

### 2. How a Request is Associated with a Tenant

A request is primarily associated with a tenant through the [`X-Tenant-ID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L155) HTTP header.
*   **JWT Claim:** While the [`X-Tenant-ID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L155) header is used, the JWT access token itself contains claims about the user and potentially their default tenant, which can inform the frontend to set this header.
*   **Frontend Role:** The frontend, specifically the [`axios`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L515) instance in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/api/client.ts`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fapi%2Fclient.ts), dynamically sets authentication tokens and tenant IDs in request headers. This ensures that all API requests are correctly authenticated and routed within the multi-tenant environment (see [React/TypeScript Frontend Architecture](#project-overview-and-architecture-reacttypescript-frontend-architecture)).
*   **[`TenantContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FTenantContext.tsx#L13):** On the frontend, the [`TenantContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FTenantContext.tsx#L13) (defined in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/contexts/TenantContext.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FTenantContext.tsx)) manages the active tenant for the logged-in user. It persists the selected tenant ID in [`localStorage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fapi%2Fclient.ts#L55) and uses it to dynamically set the [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46) header for API requests. This context also provides information about the user's role within the active tenant, which can be used to enforce client-side access control or customize UI (see [Multi-Tenancy Management Context](#frontend-global-state-contexts-multi-tenancy-management-context)).

### 3. Tenant Isolation Enforcement

Tenant isolation is enforced through a combination of middleware and explicit query filtering in handlers.
*   **[`middleware.NewTenantMiddleware`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L255):** This middleware intercepts incoming requests, identifies the tenant associated with the request (likely from [`X-Tenant-ID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L155) header or JWT claims), and stores this information in the request context. Subsequent handlers can then access this tenant context.
*   **[`middleware.RequireRootTenant()`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L660) and [`middleware.RequireRole`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L549):** For administrative or sensitive operations, specific middlewares are applied. [`middleware.RequireRootTenant()`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L660) ensures that only requests originating from the system's root tenant can access certain routes. [`middleware.RequireRole`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L549) enforces role-based access control, checking if the authenticated user has the necessary permissions within the active tenant to perform an action (e.g., [`models.RoleAdmin`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L554), [`models.RoleOwner`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L549)). This is seen in the [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/cmd/server/main.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go) file where admin API routes use [`adminAPI.Use(middleware.RequireRootTenant())`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L660) and [`adminAPI.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleAdmin))`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Ftesthelpers_test.go#L155).
*   **Handler-level Filtering:** Within individual handlers, database queries explicitly filter by [`tenantId`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Ftypes%2Findex.ts#L46). For example, in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/admin.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go), when listing tenants for the admin dashboard, the [`ListTenants`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go#L110) function constructs a [`filter`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2FREADME.md#L204) to find tenants based on query parameters, and similarly for users in [`ListUsers`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go#L502). For tenant-specific operations, like [`GetTenant`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go#L390) or [`UpdateTenantStatus`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fadmin.go#L455), the [`tenantID`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fsyslog%2Fsyslog.go#L216) from the URL is used in [`FindOne`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Fcmd%2Fserver%2Fmain.go#L786) queries. The `isolation_test.go` file [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/isolation_test.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fisolation_test.go) contains tests specifically verifying that a non-root admin cannot access resources outside their tenant, reinforcing this isolation.

### 4. White-Label Branding System

The white-label branding system allows dynamic customization of the application's appearance.
*   **Branding Model:** The [`BrandingConfig`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L22) struct in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/models/branding.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go) is the central model for branding settings. It includes fields for [`AppName`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L26), [`Tagline`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L27), [`LogoMode`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L28), [`PrimaryColor`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L31), various other color properties, [`FontFamily`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L36), [`HeadingFont`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L37), [`LandingHTML`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L43), [`DashboardHTML`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L46), [`LoginHeading`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L49), [`CustomCSS`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L55), [`HeadHTML`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L56), [`OgImageURL`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L59), and [`NavItems`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L62). This model is responsible for defining the visual identity and custom content (see [Branding and Content Management Models](#core-data-models-and-validation-branding-and-content-management-models)). [`BrandingAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L69) in the same file stores binary data like logos and favicons.

*   **Frontend Loading and Injection:**
    *   **[`BrandingContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L40):** On the frontend, the [`BrandingContext`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L40) (defined in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/contexts/BrandingContext.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx)) manages and distributes the application's branding configurations. A [`BrandingProvider`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L46) fetches this configuration from the backend, making it available to components via the [`useBranding`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcontexts%2FBrandingContext.tsx#L72) hook. A default branding configuration serves as a fallback. (See [Branding Configuration Context](#frontend-global-state-contexts-branding-configuration-context)).
    *   **[`BrandingThemeInjector`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcomponents%2FBrandingThemeInjector.tsx#L61):** The [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/frontend/src/components/BrandingThemeInjector.tsx`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Ffrontend%2Fsrc%2Fcomponents%2FBrandingThemeInjector.tsx) component is responsible for dynamically applying these branding configurations to the frontend. It injects styles, meta-information, and scripts into the HTML document, customizing elements like color palettes, fonts, page titles, favicons, analytics snippets, and custom CSS/HTML. It generates a full shade palette from a primary color and includes sanitization to prevent XSS risks during HTML/CSS injection. (See [Dynamic Branding and Theming Injection](#reusable-frontend-components-dynamic-branding-and-theming-injection)).

*   **Backend Storage and Serving:**
    *   **Storage:** The [`BrandingConfig`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L22) is stored in the MongoDB database, managed by the [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/branding.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go) handler. This handler interacts with the [`BrandingConfig`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L22) and [`BrandingAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fmodels%2Fbranding.go#L69) collections in the database.
    *   **Serving Branding:** The [`BrandingHandler`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L26) in [`/jonradoff/lastsaas/backend/internal/api/handlers/branding.go`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go) provides public-facing API endpoints to retrieve the branding configuration ([`GetBranding`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L42)) and serve branding assets like logos and favicons ([`ServeAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L110)). It also handles serving general media library files ([`ServeMedia`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L133)) and custom public pages ([`GetPublicPage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L152), [`ListPublicPages`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L169)). Administrative endpoints are available for updating the branding configuration ([`UpdateBranding`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L194)), uploading/deleting assets ([`UploadAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L266), [`DeleteAsset`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L334)), and managing custom pages ([`CreatePage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L515), [`UpdatePage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L551), [`DeletePage`](%2Fjonradoff%2Flastsaas%2Fbackend%2Finternal%2Fapi%2Fhandlers%2Fbranding.go#L602)). (See [Branding and Dynamic Content Management](#backend-api-endpoints-branding-and-dynamic-content-management)).
